items.title: Oxford physicists achieve first-ever “quadsqueezing” breakthrough in quantum physics
items.summary: Scientists have created a powerful new way to control quantum systems, achieving the first-ever demonstration of quadsqueezing—an elusive fourth-order quantum effect. By combining simple forces in a clever way, they made previously hidden quantum behaviors visible and usable, opening new frontiers f

items.title: This 275-million-year-old animal had a twisted jaw like nothing alive today
items.summary: Deep in a dried-up riverbed in Brazil, scientists uncovered a bizarre prehistoric mystery—twisted jawbones from a strange, long-lost animal unlike anything seen before. Dating back 275 million years, this creature, named Tanyka amnicola, belonged to an ancient lineage that should have already faded

items.title: Scientists reveal the best exercise for knee arthritis pain relief
items.summary: A major review of 217 trials shows that aerobic exercise is the most effective option for managing knee osteoarthritis. Activities like walking, cycling, and swimming outperformed other exercise types in reducing pain and improving movement. While alternatives like strength training and mind-body ex

items.title: This AI knew the answers but didn’t understand the questions
items.summary: For decades, psychologists have debated whether the human mind can be explained by one unified theory or must be broken into separate parts like memory and attention. items.title: First ever talks to ditch fossil fuels as UN deadlock deepens
items.summary: Some 60 countries are meeting in Colombia as frustration grows with lack of progress in tackling climate change
